noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Gap Inc.'s technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Gap Inc. best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Gap Inc.'s agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: The AI program at Gap Inc. sits inside a multi-brand retailer with thousands of stores, large digital channels and global product-development and supply-chain operations. In that setting, a frontier model has to be useful across knowledge work and software delivery without creating an unmanageable decision surface. Anthropic gives the technology organization one platform for reasoning over customer profiles, product attributes, design files, pricing histories, campaign performance and store-level inventory, orchestrating bounded agents and assisting development across commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications. Claude's focus on steerability and predictable behavior is especially relevant where privacy, brand safety, payment security and consistent controls across brands cannot be bolted on later. The result would be a safer path from isolated copilots to faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context.
cases: Reason across customer profiles, product attributes, design files, pricing histories, campaign performance and store-level inventory without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: Across Gap Inc., many small observations arise during design-to-shelf, campaign-to-conversion and omnichannel fulfillment workflows and are easy to lose between the field, floor, meeting or call and the system of record. Wispr Flow can convert those spoken inputs into clean text and commands directly inside commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications. Because it is an input layer rather than another application, adoption does not require teams to change where work is completed. The strategic fit is simple: capture more context from designers, merchants, planners, marketers, engineers and store teams, with less typing, so downstream service and AI systems begin with a more complete record.
cases: Turn store walks, fit sessions, vendor discussions, customer feedback and field observations into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: Gap Inc. is a multi-brand retailer with thousands of stores, large digital channels and global product-development and supply-chain operations, but many critical interactions still happen through web interfaces rather than clean service integrations. Browserbase gives the technology team an infrastructure layer for agents to use marketplace consoles, vendor portals, carrier sites, store tools and legacy retail applications with controlled sessions and repeatable execution. It can also parallelize browser testing for commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications, catching failures before they reach designers, merchants, planners, marketers, engineers and store teams. That makes it a useful bridge between near-term automation and longer-term modernization by removing small web-based bottlenecks that stand in the way of faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across marketplace consoles, vendor portals, carrier sites, store tools and legacy retail applications without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Gap Inc.'s existing use of Coder makes this an expansion question rather than a greenfield pitch. As the company builds an AI-ready retail platform on Google Cloud, standardized cloud development environments can become the controlled workspace for the teams changing commerce, product creation, marketing and store systems. Coder can keep source code and dependencies off local machines, reproduce complex toolchains and make onboarding easier across brands and distributed teams. The next step is to align those workspaces with Gap's AI and data platform guardrails so developer velocity rises without fragmenting security or cloud spend.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: For the technology organization, AI-agent governance becomes an identity problem before it becomes a model problem. Gap Inc. is a multi-brand retailer with thousands of stores, large digital channels and global product-development and supply-chain operations; adding commerce services, CI/CD accounts, marketing automations and AI agents increases the number of principals acting at machine speed. Token Security provides the discovery, ownership, least-privilege and audit layer required to keep that population controlled. Applied across Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate, it can show where credentials are over-permissioned or orphaned and make access changes traceable. That gives Gap Inc. a controlled path toward faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context and avoids an invisible tier of privileged automation.
cases: Inventory commerce services, CI/CD accounts, marketing automations and AI agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific customer, product, pricing, marketing, RFID and inventory data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: For Gap Inc., multimodal AI can make design-to-shelf, campaign-to-conversion and omnichannel fulfillment workflows more visual, interactive and accessible. fal provides the infrastructure to run image, video, audio and voice models at production speed without requiring every product team to manage GPU serving. That could support product imagery, campaign video, localized content and virtual merchandising assets across internal tools and external experiences, with the application retaining control over data, approvals and user context. The strategic benefit is a reusable inference layer that lets teams test more ideas, standardize deployment and reserve custom infrastructure work for the workloads that truly require it.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new shopping, product-creation, marketing and store-execution experiences.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Gap Inc.'s AI and operating decisions increasingly depend on customer, product, pricing, marketing, RFID and inventory data. A broken pipeline, silent schema change or weak model input can therefore surface as a service, safety or operational problem rather than a data-team ticket. Monte Carlo matters because it unifies observability across data pipelines, systems, code, models and the agents built on top. The technology organization would gain a common view of lineage, incidents and downstream impact across personalization, product creation, marketing optimization and inventory agents. That trust layer is central to faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context, especially where early detection and clear ownership are essential to managing privacy, brand safety, payment security and consistent controls across brands.
cases: Monitor customer, product, pricing, marketing, RFID and inventory data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context.

tonic.pull: For Gap Inc., better test data can improve both delivery speed and production safety. Tonic can create high-fidelity synthetic versions of customer, loyalty, transaction, product, pricing and inventory data, allowing developers to exercise commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications against realistic volumes and relationships without relying on raw production data. The same approach can support model training, fine-tuning and evaluation for personalization, product creation, marketing optimization and inventory agents. The strategic fit is strongest where data provisioning is slowed by privacy, brand safety, payment security and consistent controls across brands, yet inadequate test coverage would expose the organization to even greater operational or customer risk.
cases: Replace production copies of customer, loyalty, transaction, product, pricing and inventory data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Gap Inc. is a multi-brand retailer with thousands of stores, large digital channels and global product-development and supply-chain operations. That scale creates a persistent infrastructure-data problem: asset records, topology and configuration intent are often split across tools and local teams. NetBox Labs can centralize that context for stores, distribution centers, offices, cloud services and partner networks and expose it programmatically to operations and automation. That is useful for everyday network changes, but also for the agentic future—the safest infrastructure agent is one grounded in an authoritative model of devices, sites, addresses and dependencies. For Gap Inc., that supports faster change with less operational ambiguity.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across stores, distribution centers, offices, cloud services and partner networks.
Drive provisioning and change validation for Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: As Gap Inc. develops new shopping, product-creation, marketing and store-execution experiences, model serving can become a hidden bottleneck: teams compete for accelerators, batch jobs wait, and each application invents its own routing logic. Valar centralizes those concerns inside the customer's VPC. By optimizing asynchronous, batch and agentic inference, it can support personalization, product creation, marketing optimization and inventory agents with better utilization and policy consistency. The strategic value for the technology organization is control—capacity, prompts, outputs, logs and models stay inside the same boundary used for Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate.
cases: Run personalization, product creation, marketing optimization and inventory agents inside Gap Inc.'s own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: Software supply-chain risk at Gap Inc. begins with what applications and agents are built on. Echo provides CVE-free, maintained alternatives for container bases, libraries, VMs, Helm charts and end-of-life components, removing known vulnerability work at the source. Applied to commerce, product, marketing, store, mobile and data-platform software, it would let engineering and security teams spend less time patching the same underlying package across many products. That supports faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context while strengthening the evidence around consumer privacy, payments, cross-border data, brand controls and third-party risk, particularly for workloads that need hardened or FIPS-aligned components.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across commerce, product, marketing, store, mobile and data-platform software.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: Gap Inc.'s current agenda—building an AI-ready retail platform with Google Cloud across product creation, pricing, marketing, personalization and RFID-enabled inventory visibility—will increase the number of connections among applications, data services and AI agents faster than traditional firewall review can keep pace. Act Security maps those relationships and highlights communication that is unnecessary or inconsistent with business purpose. Applied to Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate, it could guide safe, incremental perimeter changes around the systems handling customer, product, pricing, marketing, RFID and inventory data. The result is enforceable least privilege that reflects the real communication paths behind design-to-shelf, campaign-to-conversion and omnichannel fulfillment workflows, rather than a static policy that becomes obsolete after the next release.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate.
Create least-privilege perimeters around commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: As Gap Inc. expands commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications, security decisions need to move upstream into the planning of new shopping, product-creation, marketing and store-execution experiences. Clover Security matters because it brings product-security context into the tools where requirements and designs are already being discussed, using sources such as Jira, Confluence and Drive to identify risk before implementation. That would help designers, merchants, planners, marketers, engineers and store teams receive concrete security guidance while changes are still inexpensive to make. The result is a design process that treats privacy, brand safety, payment security and consistent controls across brands as an input to product work rather than a late release gate.
cases: Review new shopping, product-creation, marketing and store-execution experiences while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: Gap Inc. has to prove trust across consumer privacy, payments, cross-border data, brand controls and third-party risk while its technology estate and third-party relationships continue to change. Drata matters because it turns controls, evidence, risks and assurance signals into a continuously monitored trust graph rather than a periodic collection exercise. The technology organization could use it to connect policies and owners to the systems behind commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications, surface control drift and reuse evidence across overlapping reviews. That makes compliance more operational and gives leadership a current view of the risks that could slow progress toward faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across commerce, product-creation, planning, marketing, loyalty and store applications and Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate.
Reuse assurance work across consumer privacy, payments, cross-border data, brand controls and third-party risk instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: Cloud and AI assets at Gap Inc. will change faster as the organization pursues its current agenda: building an AI-ready retail platform with Google Cloud across product creation, pricing, marketing, personalization and RFID-enabled inventory visibility. Orca Security discovers that estate agentlessly, maps the relationships among workloads, identities, data and vulnerabilities, and ranks risk by likely impact. Applied to Google Cloud-centered retail, data and AI workloads connected to a large store estate, it can give security and platform teams a shared picture of the exposures affecting customer, product, pricing, marketing, RFID and inventory data. The result is fewer blind spots, less alert fatigue and a remediation plan grounded in actual attack paths—all in service of faster product and marketing decisions with more accurate, real-time inventory context.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Gap Inc.'s cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Gap Inc. data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
